retrieved_docs.title: Endodontics, Pulp Space Anatomy and Access Cavity of Anterior Teeth -- Classification -- Morphological classification
retrieved_docs.content: Morphological classification distinguishes pulp chambers based on their form. Oval-shaped pulp chambers are frequently found in mandibular incisors and canines, characterized by elongated mesiodistal dimensions and typically containing a single root canal. Round or conical pulp chambers appear more often in maxillary central incisors, where the cavity lies centrally and narrows smoothly into a single root canal.

retrieved_docs.title: Ameloblastoma -- Evaluation
retrieved_docs.content: Tumor and characteristics are assessed using the patient’s history, clinical exam, and imaging, most commonly computed tomography (CT). On CT scan, ameloblastomas appear as radiolucent (radiographically dark) lesions of the maxilla or mandible, often with multilocular or “soap-bubble” appearance, bony expansion and tooth root resorption. They may also be associated with unerupted teeth. A definitive diagnosis of ameloblastoma is with a surgical biopsy, which shows characteristic histologic findings. It is essential to differentiate the entity from a primary intraosseous squamous cell carcinoma. The World Health Organization (WHO) classified malignant ameloblastoma into two types [3] :

retrieved_docs.title: Endodontics, Pulp Space Anatomy and Access Cavity of Anterior Teeth -- Classification
retrieved_docs.content: Selection of an access cavity should strike a balance between structural preservation and adequate canal debridement. Classification of both the pulp chamber and access cavity design enables endodontists to tailor their approach to individual tooth anatomy, thereby reducing procedural complications while maintaining long-term tooth strength.

retrieved_docs.title: Endodontics, Pulp Space Anatomy and Access Cavity of Anterior Teeth -- Classification -- Complexity-based classification
retrieved_docs.content: Complexity-based classification focuses on the following internal variations: Simple pulp chambers : Simple pulp chambers contain a single, straight canal without notable accessory branches, making them more straightforward to access and prepare. Complex pulp chambers : Complex pulp chambers may include canal bifurcations, accessory canals, or irregular chamber floors. Although less common in anterior teeth, such complexity requires magnification and advanced imaging to ensure thorough assessment and prevent overlooked anatomy.

retrieved_docs.title: Developmental Disturbances of the Teeth, Anomalies of Structure -- History and Physical
retrieved_docs.content: Dens invaginatus has various clinical presentations, which depend on the severity of the in-folding or the phase of morphodifferentiation where the invagination occurs. Several classifications exist to describe the extent of the in-folded tissue. The most utilized system is the Oehlers classification due to its ease of application. [18] In Type 1, the invagination ends as a blind sac and is limited to the coronal aspect of the tooth. Type 2 invagination extends beyond the CEJ and is retained within the main root canal of the tooth. Type 3 occurs when the invagination of dental tissue extends throughout the interior aspect of the root canal, sometimes even reaching the apical third of the tooth, potentially resulting in multiple apical foramina. [19]

retrieved_docs.title: Cracked Tooth Syndrome -- Prognosis
retrieved_docs.content: Lee et al reported a 91% pulp survival rate in cracked teeth stabilized with bidirectional splinting, while Guthrie et al reported an 11% failure rate in crowned cracked teeth necessitating endodontic therapy. [27] [30] Tan et al.'s study indicated that teeth with extensive cracks reaching the pulp, necessitating endodontic therapy and definitive cuspal coverage, exhibit a diminished prognosis and are prone to eventual failure, often resulting in extraction. [33]

retrieved_docs.title: Histology, Tooth -- Clinical Significance
retrieved_docs.content: Lateral canals can be present in different numbers in a certain tooth and cause problems during endodontic or root therapies. Radiopaque materials can be used to indicate the number and position of these canals.

retrieved_docs.content: Properties of an ideal sealer material: Ability to establish a fluid-tight seal Radiopaque (to be visible on radiographs) Antimicrobial Non-shrinking upon setting Non-staining Non-irritating to periapical tissues Insoluble in tissue fluid Biocompatible [1] [8]

retrieved_docs.title: Endodontics, Pulp Space Anatomy and Access Cavity of Anterior Teeth -- Indications
retrieved_docs.content: The TrussAC is recommended when maintaining structural integrity is paramount, yet entry into multiple canals is required. This approach is most applicable in situations where canal orientations allow separate, small entry points and where restorative longevity and functional durability are key considerations.

retrieved_docs.title: Stereognosis -- Definition/Introduction
retrieved_docs.content: The first-order neurons of the DCMLT have cell bodies in dorsal root ganglia with peripheral processes extending out to sensory receptors (Pacinian corpuscles, Merkel cells, Golgi tendon organs, and muscle spindles) and central processes that enter the spinal cord in the large sensory fiber entry zone of the dorsal horn ascending ipsilaterally in the dorsal columns. [2]

retrieved_docs.title: Dental Materials: Biodentine, a Calcium Silicate Bioactive -- Definition/Introduction
retrieved_docs.content: Biodentine has emerged in recent years as an innovative bioactive dentine substitute. Its superior physical properties, improved handling, and various clinical applications have made biodentine popular in dentistry. It has been advocated for vital pulp therapy, root perforations, resorptions, retrograde fillings, and dentin replacement. Biodentine is calcium silicate-based cement formed by highly purified tricalcium silicate (80.1%). [1] Unlike mineral trioxide aggregate (MTA), it does not contain bismuth oxide, which is known to delay setting, affecting biocompatibility and causing discoloration. [1]

retrieved_docs.title: Ghon Complex -- Definition/Introduction
retrieved_docs.content: On pathological examination, the Ghon complex usually measures approximately 1.0 to 1.5 centimeters in area. [4] A fibroblastic rim surrounds caseous necrosis. [4] Histological examination reveals granulomas with surrounding macrophages and Langerhans giant cells. [4]

retrieved_docs.title: Cracked Tooth Syndrome -- Prognosis
retrieved_docs.content: Early detection plays a crucial role in enhancing the prognosis of a cracked tooth. What initially appears as a minor, innocuous crack with mild symptoms can quickly develop into a complicated and extensive crack involving the pulp or root, rendering the tooth unrestorable. The extent, position, and direction of the crack determine the prognosis. Cracked teeth with normal pulp can be effectively managed with therapeutic measures such as direct composite splinting, placement of orthodontic bands, or indirect definitive cuspal coverage.

retrieved_docs.title: Corneal Reflex -- Definition/Introduction
retrieved_docs.content: The cornea of the human eye is the most densely innervated part of the body, 300 to 600 times more sensitive than skin. [3] It receives sensory innervation from the long ciliary branches of the ophthalmic division of the trigeminal nerve (V). [2] Branches from these nerves form nerve plexuses running parallel to the surface of the cornea between the epithelium and Bowman’s layers. [4] [5] Sensory nerve fibers within the cornea include myelinated A-beta and A-gamma nerves and unmyelinated C fibers. [6] [2]

retrieved_docs.title: Oral Surgery, Extraction of Unerupted Teeth -- Anatomy and Physiology -- Winter classification
retrieved_docs.content: The Winter classification describes mandibular 3rd molars based on the angulation of the crown with respect to the long axis of the 2nd molar. The Winter classification is as follows: Vertical Mesioangular Distoangular Horizontal Buccolingual Inverted Other Orientation Horizontal and distoangular impactions are more difficult to extract, as the path of delivery of the tooth is obstructed by overlying bone and the ascending ramus.

retrieved_docs.content: Internal and combined version without extraction is a specific form of internal obstetrical version; it represents an obstetric maneuver in which the fetus is turned internally without extraction and is classified under the broader category of internal obstetrical versions.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 0 -- Level 4 -- Community 2908
retrieved_docs.content: Combined obstetrical version is a category of obstetric procedures, and internal and combined version without extraction is a specific type within this category, indicating that the latter is a subclass of the former. This relationship clarifies that internal and combined version without extraction falls under the broader concept of combined obstetrical version.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 16 -- Level 3 -- Community 2294
retrieved_docs.content: Combined obstetrical version is a broad category of obstetric procedures that encompasses the specific technique known as internal and combined version without extraction. The internal and combined version without extraction is identified as a subtype of combined obstetrical version and is classified under the broader group of other delivery procedures, indicating its role as an alternative method used in the management of childbirth.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 6 -- Level 3 -- Community 2106
retrieved_docs.content: COMPLICATIONS MAINLY RELATED TO PREGNANCY is categorized as a specific type of maternal condition that can complicate pregnancy, childbirth, or the puerperium, and it is considered a subcategory under the broader classification of other conditions or status of the mother complicating these reproductive processes.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 30 -- Level 4 -- Community 2732
retrieved_docs.content: Other specified conditions related to pregnancy comprise a range of distinct gestational disorders. This category includes renal disorder during pregnancy, peripheral neuropathy during pregnancy, and polymorphic eruption of pregnancy, each representing a separate organ‑system complication that can arise in the context of gestation and is classified under the broader label of other specified pregnancy‑related conditions.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 26 -- Level 4 -- Community 2885
retrieved_docs.content: COMPLICATIONS MAINLY RELATED TO PREGNANCY is classified as a subcategory within the broader group of Complications of pregnancy, childbirth and the puerperium, indicating that these health issues are primarily associated with the gestational period and its immediate aftermath.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 2 -- Level 4 -- Community 2908
retrieved_docs.content: Other and ill-defined heart disease is synonymous with تمدد الأوعية الدموية في القلب, a term that describes dilation of the blood vessels within the heart. This equivalence indicates that the ambiguous classification of certain cardiac conditions corresponds to a pathological enlargement of the coronary or myocardial vasculature, which can affect cardiac function and warrants appropriate diagnostic evaluation and management.
